q: I (give) you the book tomorrow.
a: I shall give you the book tomorrow.

q: They (wait) at the cinema for us.
a: They will wait at the cinema for us.

q: Look out! The tree (fall).
a: Look out! The tree will fall.

q: If you work hard, you (pass) the examination.
a: If you work hard, you will pass the examination.

q: I (not bring) my pet to school.
a: I shall not bring my pet to school.

q: If she comes home late, she (miss) dinner.
a: If she comes home late, she will miss dinner.

q: We (sweep) the room tomorrow afternoon.
a: We shall sweep the room tomorrow afternoon.

q: When it's time to go home, she (ring) the bell.
a: When it's time to go home, she will ring the bell.

q: The girls (sing) again tonight.
a: The girls will sing again tonight.

q: (she meet) us there on Monday?
a: Will she meet us there on Monday?

q: We (not start) the meeting until he arrives.
a: We will not start the meeting until he arrives.

q: I (be) fourteen yeas old in September.
a: I shall be fourteen yeas old in September.

q: (you post) this letter for me, please?
a: Will you post this letter for me, please?

q: He (swim) in the pool tomorrow.
a: He will swim in the pool tomorrow.

q: Please wait for a while. She (come) down in a minute.
a: Please wait for a while. She will come down in a minute.

q: Christmas Day (fall) on a Sunday next year.
a: Christmas Day will fall on a Sunday next year.

q: I hope I (not forget) to bring the book tomorrow.
a: I hope I shall not forget to bring the book tomorrow.

q: He (tell) you the story this evening.
a: He will tell you the story this evening.

q: If we ask U Aung Hla, he (tell) us the story.
a: If we ask U Aung Hla, he will tell us the story.

q: I (write) a letter this evening.
a: I shall write a letter this evening.

q: I think it (rain) in the afternoon.
a: I think it will rain in the afternoon.

q: We (run) in the race tomorrow.
a: We shall run in the race tomorrow.

q: Tomorrow (be) a holiday; all the shops (be closed).
a: Tomorrow will be a holiday; all the shops will be closed.

q: The shop (open) at eight tomorrow morning.
a: The shop will open at eight tomorrow morning.

q: (he get) well soon?
a: Will he get well soon?

q: (we go) home in a taxi?
a: Shall we go home in a taxi?

q: She (bake) a cake tomorrow.
a: She will bake a cake tomorrow.

q: I (wash) the car tomorrow morning.
a: I shall wash the car tomorrow morning.

q: When (I see) you again?
a: When shall I see you again?

q: I (not wait) for you this evening.
a: I shall not wait for you this evening.
